text: Opinion builds a product. Research builds a business.
All Product teams add features. The rare ones add them with enough certainty to know why.
The biggest competitive edge isn't building something good — it's knowing you're building the right thing.
Everything is a product to me, including the very process a team uses to understand its users.
Change that doesn't account for people isn't change. It's disruption with a timeline.
Bringing Engineering and Data Science closer to customers isn't a technical problem. It's a cultural one.
AI agents don't replace the need for good questions. They make bad questions more expensive.
A dashboard no one acts on is just a very expensive mirror.
Certainty is a product you build iteratively, the same way you build anything else worth having.
The feedback loop is the product. Everything else is just output.
